narrative.summary: You're in a residential pocket where Kensington bleeds into Bathurst, walking past a house you've probably passed a hundred times without noticing. Inside, owner Regg Hart has built a 30-seat screening room that feels like the most sincere film space in the city—serious international cinema where the phone stays in your pocket or you leave. 33 Google reviews means most people who've been here feel protective of it.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in a residential pocket where Kensington bleeds into Bathurst, walking past a house you've probably passed a hundred times without noticing. Inside, owner Regg Hart has built a 30-seat underground screening room lined with vintage horror masks, movie posters floor-to-ceiling, and collectibles that feel more like a cinematic cabinet of curiosities than a theatre. The light is dim, the seats are close, and you're surrounded by decades of film obsession made physical.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This isn't about food—Cineforum is a screening room, not a restaurant. You come for international and experimental cinema programming that shifts weekly: golden-age horror one weekend, silent classics like Metropolis or a remastered Nosferatu synced to Radiohead the next. Hart introduces each film himself, bookending screenings with context and passion that makes every night feel curated for the 33 people who know this place exists.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: You want a weekend screening that reminds you cinema can still feel like a secret—horror fanatics on Friday, silent film devotees on Wednesday, anyone tired of multiplex sameness any night Hart's programming something strange and perfect.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Just found a gem in Toronto! For all the classic horror movies fanatics, this is your place! I have probably walked past that house so many times but I had no idea what was inside! The owner Regg Hart has created this incredible cinematic universe with a stunning set of collectibles, movie posters and vintage masks as decoration! Every weekend he is hosting movies from the golden age of horror, I had the chance to watch his remastered piece of the famous movie “Nosferatu” mixed with the music of Radiohead, it was just the perfect fit! This is definitely not the last time I go there and I recommend it! Regg Hart is always there and he is so welcoming, he also gives a small presentation at the start and end of the movie!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Came here on a Wednesday to see Metropolis, and it was one of the most unique experiences I’ve ever had in Toronto. The theatre is set up in Reg’s living room - there’s a huge screen and a ton of cool decor. Reg does a talk at the beginning and during the intermission of the movie - mostly about his life and experiences in the industry. If you’re expecting an in-depth analysis of the film, that’s not what this is, but you will get his pov on some of the main themes.

Reg composes his own score for the movie - intermingling the new music with the film music - so if you’re looking to watch with the original audio, keep that in mind. The new score definitely added to the unique experience.

All in all, I’d recommend Cineforum if you’re looking for a interesting movie-watching experience in the city. It wasn’t my cup of tea, but I’m really glad I went!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "This is the best cinema in Toronto. Intimate and lovingly curated. Not fancy, maybe a little worn, but done proper. Reg Hartt is a tyrant (he will kick you out if he feels you don't deserve to be there), but he is the most knowledgeable person in the business. This theatre is an institution and a gem of the city."
